testModifications.reasoning: No test files were modified by the implementer. All 29 tests in TicketCard.spec.ts pass without modification.

criteria.criterion: AC1: components/TicketCard.vue created and accepts a typed ticket prop
criteria.details: File created at apps/web/components/TicketCard.vue. Uses defineProps<{ ticket: Ticket }>() with full TypeScript interface definition (id, ref, title, type, priority, assignee).

criteria.criterion: AC2: ref rendered in monospace font
criteria.details: Line 57 renders ticket.ref in <span> with class='font-mono'. Element displays reference in monospace font as required.

criteria.criterion: AC3: type Badge uses red border for BUG and blue border for ENHANCEMENT
criteria.details: Lines 60-65 render type Badge. typeBadgeClass() function (lines 24-28) returns 'border-red-300 text-red-700' for BUG and 'border-blue-300 text-blue-700' for ENHANCEMENT.

criteria.criterion: AC4: priority Badge uses correct variant: CRITICAL=red, HIGH=orange, MEDIUM=secondary, LOW=outline
criteria.details: Lines 66-71 render priority Badge. priorityVariant() (lines 30-35) returns: CRITICAL='destructive' (red), HIGH='outline', MEDIUM='secondary', LOW='outline'. priorityClass() (lines 37-40) adds 'bg-orange-100 text-orange-800 border-orange-300' for HIGH priority.

criteria.criterion: AC5: Assignee Avatar rendered only when ticket.assignee is present
criteria.details: Lines 74-78 use <Avatar v-if='ticket.assignee'> for conditional rendering. Avatar only displays when assignee exists. Uses AvatarFallback with computed initials from assignee.name.

criteria.criterion: AC6: Component emits 'open' event when clicked
criteria.details: Line 22 defines defineEmits<{ (e: 'open'): void }>() with 'open' event. Line 53 has @click='emit("open")' on Card element. Clicking the card emits open event.

notes: Code is clean and follows Vue 3 composition API best practices
Proper TypeScript typing with no 'any' types - all props and functions have explicit types
Helper functions are concise and focused (all under 30 lines)
Uses Shadcn-nuxt components correctly (Badge, Avatar, Card)
Proper responsive design with flex layout and truncation handling
Assignee initials computed correctly, handles multi-word names properly

reasoning: Implementation is complete and correct. All 29 tests pass without modification. All 6 acceptance criteria are met. Code quality is high with proper TypeScript typing, clean Vue.js patterns, and good component structure. The component integrates well with the existing Shadcn-nuxt component library and follows the project's coding conventions.
